task: Set ER605 combobox field via browser automation API
goal: Set a custom combobox value on TP-Link ER605 management UI where standard DOM selectors fail
constraints: Must click .combobox-switch before label is visible
Must use Python requests instead of curl for complex JS

rejected_agents.reason: Not a CI/CD or deployment task — this is hardware UI interaction
Not reviewing code — interacting with a live UI

plan: Identify the combobox DOM structure via evaluate endpoint
Discover #policy is hidden input with value attribute
Navigate to parentElement to get .combobox-container
Click .combobox-switch to open dropdown panel
Find label.combobox-label wrapping input[value=TARGET]
Click the label to select the option
Verify the hidden input value changed
actions: safe_shell: POST /evaluate with JS to inspect #policy element type and attributes
safe_shell: POST /evaluate to get parentElement className (combobox-container)
safe_shell: POST /evaluate to click combobox-switch child element
safe_shell: POST /evaluate to find and click label with target value
safe_shell: POST /evaluate to read #policy.value for verification
validations: Hidden input value matches expected selection
Combobox display area shows selected label text
result: Combobox value set correctly despite non-standard DOM structure
why_this_route_was_correct: Hardware management UI interaction requires infrastructure expertise. The ER605 uses custom combobox widgets that don't follow standard HTML patterns. it_agent understands hardware UI quirks.

task: Debug and fix browser automation API escaping issues
goal: Fix Internal Server Error when sending complex JavaScript expressions to browser-worker /evaluate endpoint via curl
constraints: Curl shell escaping breaks mixed single/double quotes in JS
Browser-worker runs in k8s pod on port 8082
Must maintain session cookies — never use /navigate

rejected_agents.reason: Not a deployment issue — it's an API request formatting problem
The service isn't crashed — the request is malformed

plan: Identify that curl shell escaping corrupts JS with quotes
Switch to Python requests.post() with json= parameter
json= handles escaping automatically
Verify the expression evaluates correctly
actions: safe_shell: attempted curl with escaped JS — got 500 error
safe_shell: Python requests.post(url, json={'expression': js_code}) — success
safe_shell: verified response contains expected DOM data
validations: Response body contains expected element properties
result: Switched from curl to Python requests for all browser automation calls — proper JSON escaping
why_this_route_was_correct: API interaction debugging with browser automation infrastructure is it_agent territory. The fix was about HTTP request formatting, not code quality or deployment.

task: Fill multi-field form on hardware management UI
goal: Create an ER605 ACL rule by setting name, policy, zone, source, destination, and time fields sequentially
constraints: Each combobox must be opened and selected individually
Name field uses standard input (no combobox)
Time MUST be set to 'Any' — empty causes silent validation failure
Rule names accept underscores only, no hyphens
Must verify all fields before clicking submit

rejected_agents.reason: Form filling on network hardware is infrastructure, not deployment
Not a data entry/ETL task — hardware UI interaction

plan: Click Add button to open form
Set rule name via standard input field
Set policy combobox (DROP/ACCEPT) via parentElement pattern
Set zone combobox (LAN)
Set source combobox (IP group name)
Set destination combobox (IP group name)
Set time combobox to 'Any'
Verify all 6 fields match expected values
Click submit/save button
Verify new row in ACL table
actions: safe_shell: click #add button to open form
safe_shell: for each combobox — open via .combobox-switch, click target label
safe_shell: read all field values for verification
safe_shell: read table to confirm new row
validations: All 6 fields show correct values before submit
New row visible in ACL table with correct ID
Rule name, policy, zone, source, dest, time all match spec
result: ACL rule created successfully with all fields correct
why_this_route_was_correct: Sequential form filling on network hardware management UI requires infrastructure expertise and understanding of custom UI widget patterns.

task: Verify form submission result in dynamic table
goal: After submitting an ACL rule form, verify the new entry appears in the ACL table with correct field values
constraints: Must read specific cells by row index
Table may have different column ordering than form fields

rejected_agents.reason: Not reviewing code — verifying live UI state
Not monitoring system health — verifying UI form submission

plan: After form submit, wait for table to refresh
Count table rows to find new entry
Read each cell in the new row
Compare against expected values from the form
Report pass/fail for each field
actions: safe_shell: evaluate JS to count table tbody rows
safe_shell: evaluate JS to read cells in last row
validations: All cell values match: name, policy, zone, src, dest, time
result: New ACL rule verified in table — all fields correct
why_this_route_was_correct: Table verification after form submission on hardware UI is infrastructure validation work.
